message:
style(fnp-conformance): rustfmt pass over compliance-report binaries

Pure `cargo fmt` normalization across the two CLI binaries under
`crates/fnp-conformance/src/bin/` that print / write compliance
reports. No behavioural change; no public API touched. Brings the
tree back to `cargo fmt --check` clean.

`dump_expected.rs`:
- Two `g.<dist>(args).expect("long message")` call chains were
  laid out with the `.expect(...)` hanging on its own indented
  line (as one long string). rustfmt prefers the form where
  `.expect(` is on a new line, the message is on its own line at
  outer indent + 4, and the trailing `)` closes aligned with
  `.expect(`. Applied to both the `dirichlet` and `hypergeometric`
  cases.

`generate_compliance_report.rs`:
- The per-domain `println!` with six positional arguments gets
  one-arg-per-line wrapping now that its call crosses the
  100-column limit after rustfmt's 2024-edition `{...}` inline
  formatting rules.
- Function signatures for `walk_fixtures(root, stats)` and
  `count_fixture(path, filename, stats)` collapse back to a
  single-line form; rustfmt's "short-fn-signature" heuristic fits
  under 100 cols once the body's local bindings are reflowed.
- Two `?`-chained io calls (`fs::read_dir(root).map_err(...)`,
  `fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(...)`,
  `serde_json::from_str(&content).map_err(...)`) collapse back
  to single-line form with the `.map_err(...)` directly
  following the primary call, again fitting under 100 cols.
- A `out.push_str("> Do not edit by hand ...\n")` call that was
  on one long line in the markdown-rendering block gets re-wrapped
  into a multi-line form with the string on its own indented
  line, because rustfmt counts the preceding `out.push_str(` +
  the UTF-8 text as over-width.

Verified with `cargo fmt --check -p fnp-conformance` before /
after — the diff is exactly what rustfmt wants.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
